methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $209M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Asimov, a Series B biotech company, has raised $209M to date across its founders Chris Voigt, Doug Densmore, Alec Nielsen, and Raja Srinivas. Valuation analysis relies on two applicable methodologies triangulated against a peer set of 4 private-stage companies in Biotech & Health, spanning Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B, Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B, Series B), New Limit ($3.1B, Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M, Series B).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $209M capital-raised proxy, yielding $397M. Peer-median anchoring across the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a $3.5B implied valuation, with the peer set ranging from $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring was inapplicable; while three public Biotech & Health tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) trade at a 6.9x P/S median, Asimov's revenue is not disclosed.

The two applicable methods support a triangulated range of $338M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage timing, market position relative to peers, and capital efficiency. The peer set's 19x valuation spread ($539M to $10.3B) underscores that Series B positioning alone does not constrain valuation tightly in this sector.
